name: Effect v4 Doc: Schema - Elementary and Primitive Types
episode_body:
Title: Schema Elementary and Primitive Types
Category: documentation
Source: SCHEMA.md (Section 1)
Module Path: effect/Schema

Description: The Effect v4 Schema module provides type-safe schema definitions that directly represent TypeScript types. Elementary schemas include String, Number, Boolean, BigInt, Symbol, Date, URL, Uint8Array, and literal types.

Key Concepts:
- Schemas directly represent TypeScript types in v4 (unlike v3 which focused on transformations)
- Import pattern: `import { Schema } from "effect"` (NOT from "@effect/schema" which is deprecated)
- Elementary schemas: Schema.String, Schema.Number, Schema.Boolean, Schema.BigInt, Schema.Symbol, Schema.Date, Schema.URL
- Literal schemas: Schema.Literal("value"), Schema.Literals(["a", "b"]) for unions
- Null handling: Schema.Null (not Schema.Literal(null) as in v3)
- Template literals: Schema.TemplateLiteral([Schema.String, ".", Schema.String]) (array syntax in v4)
- String refinements: Schema.NonEmptyString, Schema.Trimmed, Schema.Lowercased, Schema.Uppercased, Schema.Capitalized
- Number refinements: Schema.Int, Schema.Finite, Schema.NonNaN, Schema.Positive, Schema.Negative
- Date schemas: Schema.Date (runtime Date), Schema.ValidDate (non-Invalid Date), Schema.ValidDateFromString (from ISO string)
- FiniteFromString, NumberFromString for string-to-number transformations
- NullOr, UndefinedOr combinators for nullable/optional wrappers
- Option, Result, Exit schemas for Effect data types

Since: effect v4 beta

name: Effect v4 Doc: Schema - Structs and Composite Types
episode_body:
Title: Schema Structs, Tuples, Arrays, Records, and Unions
Category: documentation
Source: SCHEMA.md (Section 2)
Module Path: effect/Schema

Description: Schema v4 provides composite type constructors for building complex data structures from elementary schemas, including structs with optional/mutable keys, tuples, arrays, records, and discriminated unions.

Key Concepts:

Structs:
- Schema.Struct({ key: SchemaType }) for object schemas
- Schema.optionalKey(schema) for `key?: T` (key may be absent)
- Schema.optional(schema) for `key?: T | undefined` (key present but undefined allowed)
- Schema.mutableKey(schema) for mutable fields
- Default values via Schema.withDefault(() => value) or Schema.withConstructorDefault(() => value)
- Index signatures via Schema.indexSignature(keySchema, valueSchema)
- Struct field manipulation: .mapFields(Struct.pick(["a"])), .mapFields(Struct.omit(["b"])), .mapFields(Struct.map(Schema.optional))
- Schema.fieldsAssign({ newField: Schema.String }) to extend structs
- Schema.annotate({ description, title }) for metadata
- Schema.annotateKey({ description, missingKeyMessage }) for per-field metadata

Tuples:
- Schema.Tuple([Schema.String, Schema.Number]) - array syntax in v4
- Optional elements, rest elements supported

Arrays:
- Schema.Array(elementSchema) for ReadonlyArray
- Schema.NonEmptyArray(elementSchema)

Records:
- Schema.Record(keySchema, valueSchema) - positional args in v4 (not named object)

Unions:
- Schema.Union([Schema.String, Schema.Number]) - array syntax in v4
- Tagged unions with Schema.tag("TypeName") auto-discriminated by _tag
- Schema.TaggedClass for discriminated union variants

Since: effect v4 beta

name: Effect v4 Doc: Schema - Validation, Filters, and Brands
episode_body:
Title: Schema Validation, Filters, Refinements, and Brands
Category: documentation
Source: SCHEMA.md (Section 3)
Module Path: effect/Schema

Description: Schema v4 provides a comprehensive validation system using .check() for adding filters, refinements for type narrowing, and brands for nominal typing.

Key Concepts:

Filters (.check):
- Schema.String.check(Schema.isMinLength(1)) - built-in filter
- Schema.String.check(Schema.isMaxLength(100), Schema.isPattern(/^[a-z]+$/))
- Schema.makeFilter(predicate, options) for custom filters
- Structural filters on structs: schema.check(Schema.makeFilter(({a, b}) => a === b))
- Multiple filters chain with .check(filter1, filter2)
- .abort() on a filter to stop validation on first failure

Built-in String Filters:
- Schema.isMinLength(n), Schema.isMaxLength(n), Schema.isLength(n)
- Schema.isPattern(regex), Schema.isNonEmpty()
- Schema.isUUID(), Schema.isULID(), Schema.isEmail(), Schema.isURL()
- Schema.isTrimmed(), Schema.isLowercased(), Schema.isUppercased(), Schema.isCapitalized()

Built-in Number Filters:
- Schema.isInt(), Schema.isFinite(), Schema.isNonNaN()
- Schema.isGreaterThan(n), Schema.isLessThan(n), Schema.isBetween({minimum, maximum})
- Schema.isPositive(), Schema.isNegative(), Schema.isNonPositive(), Schema.isNonNegative()
- Schema.isMultipleOf(n)

Refinements:
- Schema.refine(predicate) for type narrowing (unlike .check which doesn't narrow)
- Returns a refined type in TypeScript

Brands:
- Schema.brand("BrandName") for nominal typing
- Prevents mixing structurally identical types
- Brand.Brand<"Name"> type utility

Effectful Filters:
- Schema.decode({ decode: SchemaGetter.checkEffect(effectFn), encode: SchemaGetter.passthrough() })
- Used for async validation (e.g., database lookups)

Since: effect v4 beta

name: Effect v4 Doc: Schema - Transformations and Encoding
episode_body:
Title: Schema Transformations, decodeTo, and encodeTo
Category: documentation
Source: SCHEMA.md (Section 4)
Module Path: effect/Schema

Description: Schema v4 provides transformation combinators for mapping between different type representations during encoding and decoding. The primary APIs are decodeTo, encodeTo, and decode.

Key Concepts:

Core Transformation APIs:
- Schema.decodeTo(targetSchema, { decode, encode }) - transform from source to target during decode
- Schema.encodeTo(targetSchema, { decode, encode }) - transform from source to target during encode
- Schema.decode({ decode, encode }) - transform without changing schema (same type in/out)
- SchemaTransformation.transform({ decode, encode }) for pure synchronous transforms
- SchemaGetter.transform(fn) for simple one-way mappings
- SchemaGetter.transformOrFail(fn) for failable transforms returning Effect
- SchemaGetter.passthrough() for identity transforms

Composition:
- Transformations compose via piping: schema.pipe(Schema.decodeTo(A), Schema.decodeTo(B))
- Replaces v3's Schema.compose

Optional Key Management:
- SchemaGetter.withDefault(() => value) for providing defaults on missing keys
- SchemaGetter.required() for making optional keys required
- SchemaGetter.transformOptional(fn) for Option-based optional key transforms

Built-in Transformations:
- SchemaTransformation.numberFromString for NumberFromString codec
- SchemaTransformation.capitalize(), lowercase(), uppercase()
- Schema.Trim for string trimming
- Schema.fromJsonString(schema) for JSON string decode/encode

Flipping:
- Schema.flip(schema) swaps encode and decode directions
- Useful for bidirectional codec composition

Since: effect v4 beta

name: Effect v4 Doc: Schema - Classes, TaggedClass, and ErrorClass
episode_body:
Title: Schema Classes, TaggedClass, ErrorClass, and TaggedErrorClass
Category: documentation
Source: SCHEMA.md (Section 5)
Module Path: effect/Schema

Description: Schema v4 provides class-based schema definitions that combine runtime validation with TypeScript class instances, supporting inheritance, branding, recursion, and error handling.

Key Concepts:

Schema.Class:
- class A extends Schema.Class<A>("A")({ fields }) {} - defines a validated class
- Constructor validates input: new A({ field: value })
- A.makeUnsafe({ field: value }) for explicit construction
- Schema.decodeUnknownSync(A)({ field: value }) for decoding
- .extend<B>("B")({ newFields }) for inheritance
- Static members preserved with typeof Base as second generic
- Custom class fields (readonly _x = 1) allowed alongside schema fields
- Filters via .check() on struct passed to class definition
- Branded classes: Schema.Class<A, { readonly brand: unique symbol }>("A")({ fields })
- Annotations: Schema.Class<A>("A")({ fields }, { title: "my title" })

Recursive Classes:
- Use Schema.suspend((): Schema.Codec<Self> => Self) for self-reference
- Works for tree structures, mutually recursive types
- Supports different Encoded and Type with interface CategoryEncoded extends Schema.Codec.Encoded<typeof Category>

Schema.TaggedClass:
- Adds automatic _tag field using Schema.tag
- class Person extends Schema.TaggedClass<Person>()("Person", { fields }) {}
- Tag value doubles as identifier by default
- Custom identifier: Schema.TaggedClass<Person>("MyPerson")("Person", { fields })
- Perfect for discriminated unions: Schema.Union([Cat, Dog])

Schema.ErrorClass:
- class E extends Schema.ErrorClass<E>("E")({ fields }) {}
- For structured errors without tag

Schema.TaggedErrorClass:
- class HttpError extends Schema.TaggedErrorClass<HttpError>()("HttpError", { fields }) {}
- Catchable with Effect.catchTag("HttpError", handler)
- Multiple tagged errors caught with Effect.catchTags({ Tag1: handler1, Tag2: handler2 })
- All Class features available: extend, annotate, check

Since: effect v4 beta

name: Effect v4 Doc: Schema - Serialization and Canonical Codecs
episode_body:
Title: Schema Serialization: JSON, FormData, URLSearchParams, XML
Category: documentation
Source: SCHEMA.md (Section 6)
Module Path: effect/Schema

Description: Schema v4 provides canonical codecs for serializing and deserializing values to various formats including JSON, FormData, URLSearchParams, and XML. These codecs handle types that cannot safely round-trip through JSON.

Key Concepts:

JSON Support:
- Schema.UnknownFromJsonString: decodes JSON string to unknown value
- Schema.fromJsonString(schema): decodes JSON string then validates with schema
- Problem: Date, Uint8Array, Map, Set, BigInt, custom classes don't round-trip through JSON
- Solution: Canonical codecs with toCodecJson annotation

Canonical JSON Codec:
- Schema.toCodecJson(schema) produces codec that handles JSON-unsafe types
- Annotation-based: add toCodecJson annotation to schema for custom JSON representation
- Schema.link<Type>()(jsonRepresentationSchema, transformation) for custom types
- Built-in handling: Date -> ISO string, Option/Result/Exit -> tagged objects
- Custom encodings take priority over defaults
- JSON Schema generation uses same toCodecJson annotation via Schema.toJsonSchemaDocument()

FormData Support:
- Schema.fromFormData(schema): decodes FormData using bracket notation for nesting
- Nested: formData.append("b[c]", "2") maps to { b: { c: "2" } }
- Schema.toCodecStringTree with keepDeclarations: true preserves Blob values

URLSearchParams Support:
- Schema.fromURLSearchParams(schema): similar to FormData, bracket notation
- Same nesting rules as FormData

StringTree Canonical Codec:
- Schema.toCodecStringTree(schema) converts all leaf values to strings
- keepDeclarations: true preserves non-string declarations (URL, Blob)

ISO Canonical Codec:
- Schema.toCodecIso(schema) for isomorphic transformations
- Used internally for building optics

XML Encoder:
- Schema.toEncoderXml(schema) serializes values to XML
- Uses toCodecStringTree internally

Since: effect v4 beta

name: Effect v4 Doc: Schema - Tooling: JSON Schema, Arbitrary, Equivalence, Optics, Differ
episode_body:
Title: Schema Generation and Tooling: JSON Schema, Arbitrary, Equivalence, Optics, Differ
Category: documentation
Source: SCHEMA.md (Section 7)
Module Path: effect/Schema

Description: Schema v4 can derive JSON Schemas, test data generators, equivalence checks, optics, and JSON Patch differs from a single schema definition.

Key Concepts:

JSON Schema Generation:
- Schema.toJsonSchemaDocument(schema) produces draft-2020-12 JSON Schema
- JsonSchema.toDocumentDraft07(doc) converts to draft-07
- Supports annotations: title, description, default, examples, readOnly, writeOnly
- Optional fields become optional in JSON Schema
- Validation constraints (isMinLength, isMaxLength) produce allOf constraints
- fromJsonString uses contentSchema/contentMediaType
- Custom types need toCodecJson annotation for proper JSON Schema output

Arbitrary Generation (Property-Based Testing):
- Schema.toArbitrary(schema) produces fast-check Arbitrary<T>
- import { FastCheck } from "effect/testing" (NOT @fast-check/vitest)
- FastCheck.sample(arb, count) for sampling values
- Handles: primitives, tuples, structs, records, unions, template literals, recursion
- Custom type support via arbitrary annotation: Schema.instanceOf(URL, { arbitrary: () => fc => fc.webUrl().map(s => new URL(s)) })
- Override default with toArbitrary annotation
- Filter constraints via arbitraryConstraint in makeFilter options
- Faker.js integration possible via custom arbitrary annotation

Equivalence Generation:
- Schema.toEquivalence(schema) derives structural equality checker
- Custom via toEquivalence annotation on instanceOf
- Override with Schema.overrideToEquivalence()

Optic Generation:
- Schema.toIso(schema) generates Iso optic from schema
- Works with Class schemas out of the box
- Enables composable get/replace/modify on schema-typed data

JSON Patch Differ:
- Schema.toDifferJsonPatch(schema) creates type-safe JSON Patch differ
- differ.diff(oldValue, newValue) produces RFC 6902 patch operations
- differ.patch(oldValue, jsonPatch) applies patches
- Works with custom types via schema serializer

Since: effect v4 beta

name: Effect v4 Doc: Schema - SchemaRepresentation and Code Generation
episode_body:
Title: Schema Representation, Serialization, and Code Generation
Category: documentation
Source: SCHEMA.md (Section 8)
Module Path: effect/SchemaRepresentation

Description: The SchemaRepresentation module converts schemas into portable data structures for storage, network transmission, runtime rebuilding, JSON Schema generation, and TypeScript code generation.

Key Concepts:

SchemaRepresentation:
- fromAST/fromASTs: convert schema AST to Document/MultiDocument
- DocumentFromJson: round-trip documents through JSON
- toSchema: rebuild runtime Schema from stored representation
- toJsonSchemaDocument: produce Draft 2020-12 JSON Schema
- toCodeDocument: prepare data for code generation

Document Model:
- Representation: tagged object tree describing schema structure
- Document: root representation + references map for shared definitions
- MultiDocument: multiple root representations sharing references

Limitations:
- Transformations NOT supported (user code/functions can't be serialized)
- Only built-in checks can be represented (isMinLength, isPattern, isUUID, etc.)
- Custom predicates (Schema.filter) not supported
- Annotations filtered: only JSON-primitive-like values kept
- Declarations need reviver function (default handles Option, Result, Exit, Map, Set, Date, URL, etc.)

Code Generation:
- toCodeDocument sorts references for dependency-ordered emission
- Keeps recursive definitions separate (emitted with Schema.suspend)
- Sanitizes reference names into valid JS identifiers

Since: effect v4 beta

name: Effect v4 Doc: Schema - Error Handling and Formatting
episode_body:
Title: Schema Error Handling, Formatters, and Custom Messages
Category: documentation
Source: SCHEMA.md (Section 9)
Module Path: effect/SchemaIssue

Description: Schema v4 produces structured error objects on validation failure. Formatters convert these into human-readable messages for display or logging, with support for internationalization and custom messages.

Key Concepts:

StandardSchemaV1 Formatter:
- SchemaIssue.makeFormatterStandardSchemaV1(options)(issue) produces { issues: [{ message, path }] }
- Customizable via LeafHook and CheckHook functions
- LeafHook handles: InvalidType, InvalidValue, MissingKey, UnexpectedKey, Forbidden, OneOf
- CheckHook handles custom filter/refinement failures

Customizing Messages:
- Hook-based: pass leafHook and checkHook to formatter for centralized message control
- Annotation-based: .annotate({ message: "custom" }) for type mismatch messages
- .annotateKey({ missingKeyMessage: "required" }) for missing key messages
- Filter messages: Schema.isNonEmpty({ message: "must not be empty" })
- i18next integration pattern for localization

Sending Failures Over Wire:
- Schema.StandardSchemaV1FailureResult schema for serializing validation errors
- Encode with Schema.toCodecJson(Schema.StandardSchemaV1FailureResult)

Since: effect v4 beta

name: Effect v4 Doc: Schema - Middlewares and Fallbacks
episode_body:
Title: Schema Middlewares: Fallbacks, Service Integration, and catchDecoding
Category: documentation
Source: SCHEMA.md (Section 10)
Module Path: effect/Schema

Description: Schema v4 middlewares wrap around decoding/encoding to intercept errors, provide fallback values, or inject services.

Key Concepts:

Fallbacks:
- Schema.catchDecoding(() => Effect.succeedSome("fallback")) - return fallback on decode failure
- Return Effect.succeedNone to omit a field from output
- Useful for optional fields: Schema.optionalKey(Schema.String).pipe(Schema.catchDecoding(() => Effect.succeedNone))

Service-Based Fallbacks:
- Schema.catchDecodingWithContext(() => Effect.gen(function*() { ... })) for service dependencies
- Schema.revealCodec shows Codec type parameters including service requirements
- Schema.middlewareDecoding(Effect.provideService(Service, impl)) to provide services

Since: effect v4 beta

name: Effect v4 Doc: Schema - Advanced Type System and Model
episode_body:
Title: Schema Advanced: Type Model, Bottom Interface, Annotations, Generics
Category: documentation
Source: SCHEMA.md (Section 11)
Module Path: effect/Schema

Description: Advanced Schema v4 type system internals covering the Bottom interface, type hierarchy, typed annotations, and separate requirement parameters.

Key Concepts:

Schema Type Hierarchy:
- Bottom: foundation type with 15 type parameters (T, E, RD, RE, Ast, RebuildOut, TypeMakeIn, Iso, TypeParameters, etc.)
- Top: generic schema with no fixed shape
- Schema<T>: represents TypeScript type T
- Codec<T, E, RD, RE>: decodes E to T, encodes T to E, with service requirements
- Use Top/Schema/Codec as constraints only, never as explicit return types or annotations

Separate Requirement Parameters:
- RD: services required only for decoding
- RE: services required only for encoding
- Enables schemas where encoding has no dependencies even if decoding needs a database

Typed Annotations:
- SchemaAnnotations.resolveInto(schema) retrieves typed annotations
- Extend annotations via module declaration: declare module "effect/SchemaAnnotations" { interface Annotations { version?: [...] } }

Integrations:
- TanStack Form: Schema.toStandardSchemaV1(schema) for form validation
- Elysia: Schema.toStandardSchemaV1 + Schema.toJsonSchema for OpenAPI
- encodingJsonSchema/decodingJsonSchema/decodingStringSchema utility patterns

Since: effect v4 beta

name: Effect v4 Doc: Schema - Migration from v3
episode_body:
Title: Schema Migration: v3 to v4 API Changes
Category: documentation
Source: SCHEMA.md (Section 12)
Module Path: effect/Schema

Description: Comprehensive mapping of Schema v3 APIs to their v4 equivalents, covering renames, removed APIs, and pattern changes.

Key Migration Changes:

Renames:
- asSchema -> revealCodec (Schema is now Codec)
- encodedSchema -> toEncoded
- typeSchema -> toType
- BigIntFromSelf -> BigInt, SymbolFromSelf -> Symbol, URLFromSelf -> URL
- pattern -> isPattern (via .check())
- nonEmptyString -> isNonEmpty
- annotations({...}) -> annotate({...})

Decoding/Encoding API Renames:
- decodeUnknown -> decodeUnknownEffect
- decode -> decodeEffect
- decodeUnknownEither -> decodeUnknownResult
- encodeUnknown -> encodeUnknownEffect
- encode -> encodeEffect
- All *Either -> *Result (Either is now Result in v4)

Removed APIs:
- validate/validateEither/validatePromise/validateSync/validateOption -> use decode* + toType
- keyof, ArrayEnsure, NonEmptyArrayEnsure, withDefaults, fromKey -> no equivalent

Syntax Changes:
- Literal(null) -> Schema.Null
- Literal("a", "b") -> Literals(["a", "b"]) (array syntax)
- Union(A, B) -> Union([A, B]) (array syntax)
- Tuple(A, B) -> Tuple([A, B]) (array syntax)
- Record({ key, value }) -> Record(key, value) (positional args)
- TemplateLiteral(A, ".", B) -> TemplateLiteral([A, ".", B]) (array syntax)
- compose -> decodeTo
- filter(fn) -> .check(Schema.makeFilter(fn))
- transform(from, to, {decode, encode}) -> from.pipe(Schema.decodeTo(to, SchemaTransformation.transform({decode, encode})))
- transformOrFail -> decodeTo with SchemaGetter.transformOrFail
- transformLiteral(0, "a") -> Schema.Literal(0).transform("a")
- transformLiterals -> Schema.Literals([...]).transform([...])

Optional Field Changes:
- optionalWith({exact:true}) -> optionalKey
- optionalWith({default}) -> optional + decodeTo with SchemaGetter.withDefault
- optionalWith({nullable:true}) -> optional(NullOr(schema)) + decodeTo
- optionalToOptional -> optionalKey + decodeTo with SchemaGetter.transformOptional
- optionalToRequired -> optionalKey + decodeTo
- requiredToOptional -> schema + decodeTo with SchemaGetter.transformOptional

Struct Operations:
- pick("a") -> .mapFields(Struct.pick(["a"]))
- omit("b") -> .mapFields(Struct.omit(["b"]))
- partial -> .mapFields(Struct.map(Schema.optional))
- partialWith({exact:true}) -> .mapFields(Struct.map(Schema.optionalKey))
- required -> .mapFields(Struct.map(Schema.required)) or Schema.requiredKey
- extend(otherStruct) -> .mapFields(Struct.assign(fields)) or Schema.fieldsAssign(fields)
- rename({a:"c"}) -> Schema.encodeKeys({a:"c"}) (experimental) or manual decodeTo
- attachPropertySignature -> Schema.tagDefaultOmit("value")
- decodingFallback -> Schema.catchDecoding

Since: effect v4 beta

name: Effect v4 Doc: HttpApi - Overview and Getting Started
episode_body:
Title: HttpApi Overview and Getting Started
Category: documentation
Source: HTTPAPI.md (Section 1)
Module Path: effect/unstable/httpapi

Description: The Effect v4 HttpApi module provides a declarative, type-safe HTTP API framework built on the Effect ecosystem. It uses a layered architecture with endpoints, groups, and API definitions.

Key Concepts:

Architecture:
- Import: `import { HttpApi, HttpApiBuilder, HttpApiClient, HttpApiEndpoint, HttpApiGroup } from "effect/unstable/httpapi"`
- HttpApiEndpoint: defines individual routes with method, path, schemas for request/response
- HttpApiGroup: groups related endpoints together
- HttpApi: top-level API definition combining groups
- HttpApiBuilder: provides handler implementations for endpoints
- HttpApiClient: derives type-safe client from API definition

Basic Pattern:
1. Define endpoints: HttpApiEndpoint.get("name", "/path", { success: Schema })
2. Group endpoints: HttpApiGroup.make("GroupName").add(endpoint)
3. Create API: HttpApi.make("ApiName").add(group)
4. Implement handlers: HttpApiBuilder.group(Api, "GroupName", handlers => handlers.handle("name", impl))
5. Build layer: HttpApiBuilder.layer(Api).pipe(Layer.provide(GroupLive))
6. Serve: HttpRouter.serve + NodeHttpServer.layer

HTTP Methods:
- HttpApiEndpoint.get, .post, .put, .patch, .delete, .head, .options

Server Setup:
- import { NodeHttpServer, NodeRuntime } from "@effect/platform-node"
- import { HttpRouter } from "effect/unstable/http"
- Layer.launch(serverLayer).pipe(NodeRuntime.runMain)

Since: effect v4 beta

name: Effect v4 Doc: HttpApi - Routing and Path Parameters
episode_body:
Title: HttpApi Routing: Paths, Parameters, Prefixing, and Groups
Category: documentation
Source: HTTPAPI.md (Section 2)
Module Path: effect/unstable/httpapi

Description: HttpApi v4 supports flexible routing with path parameters, catch-all routes, route prefixing, and top-level group flattening.

Key Concepts:

Path Parameters:
- HttpApiEndpoint.get("name", "/users/:id") - colon prefix for path params
- Parameters decoded automatically from path
- Schema validation on path params via pathParams option

Catch-All Routes:
- HttpApiEndpoint.get("catchAll", "/*") for wildcard matching

Route Prefixing:
- HttpApiGroup.make("GroupName", { prefix: "/api/v1" }) for group-level prefixing
- Prefix applied to all endpoints in the group

Top-Level Groups:
- HttpApiGroup.make("GroupName", { topLevel: true }) flattens endpoints
- Client methods not nested under group name

Multiple Groups:
- Api.add(group1).add(group2) for composing multiple groups
- Each group independently implemented via HttpApiBuilder.group

Since: effect v4 beta

name: Effect v4 Doc: HttpApi - Request Handling
episode_body:
Title: HttpApi Request: Query, Headers, Body, Multipart, Cookies, Streaming
Category: documentation
Source: HTTPAPI.md (Section 3)
Module Path: effect/unstable/httpapi

Description: HttpApi v4 provides type-safe request handling for query parameters, headers, body encoding, multipart uploads, cookies, and streaming.

Key Concepts:

Query Parameters:
- HttpApiEndpoint.get("name", "/path", { query: Schema.Struct({...}) })
- Automatically decoded from URL query string

Path Parameters:
- HttpApiEndpoint.get("name", "/users/:id", { pathParams: Schema.Struct({ id: Schema.NumberFromString }) })

Headers:
- HttpApiEndpoint.get("name", "/path", { headers: Schema.Struct({ authorization: Schema.String }) })
- Request headers decoded and validated

Request Body:
- HttpApiEndpoint.post("name", "/path", { body: Schema.Struct({...}), success: Schema })
- JSON body by default
- body: HttpApiEndpoint.BodyUrlParams(schema) for URL-encoded forms

Multipart Upload:
- body: HttpApiEndpoint.BodyMultipart(schema) for file uploads
- HttpApiMultipart.SingleFile for single file fields
- HttpApiMultipart.FileArray for multiple files

Cookies:
- HttpApiEndpoint.get("name", "/path", { cookies: Schema.Struct({...}) })
- Cookies decoded from request headers

Streaming Request Body:
- body: HttpApiEndpoint.BodyStream for stream input
- handler receives Stream of Uint8Array

Encoding Formats:
- Default JSON encoding
- HttpApiEndpoint.BodyUrlParams for application/x-www-form-urlencoded
- HttpApiEndpoint.BodyMultipart for multipart/form-data

Since: effect v4 beta

name: Effect v4 Doc: HttpApi - Response Handling
episode_body:
Title: HttpApi Response: Status Codes, Headers, Cookies, Redirects, Streaming
Category: documentation
Source: HTTPAPI.md (Section 4)
Module Path: effect/unstable/httpapi

Description: HttpApi v4 provides declarative response definition with status codes, response headers, cookies, redirects, and streaming responses.

Key Concepts:

Success Responses:
- success: Schema defines the default 200 response
- HttpApiEndpoint.get("name", "/path", { success: Schema.String })
- Custom status: HttpApiEndpoint.setSuccess(endpoint, { status: 201, body: Schema })

Response Headers:
- HttpApiEndpoint.setSuccess(endpoint, { headers: Schema.Struct({...}) })
- Type-safe response headers

Cookies in Response:
- HttpApiEndpoint.setSuccess(endpoint, { cookies: Schema.Struct({...}) })
- Set-Cookie headers from schema

Redirects:
- HttpApiEndpoint.setSuccess(endpoint, { status: 302, headers: Schema.Struct({ location: Schema.String }) })

Streaming Responses:
- Return Stream<Uint8Array> from handler for chunked transfer
- Success schema: HttpApiEndpoint.StreamSuccess

Empty Responses:
- success: Schema.Void for 204 No Content responses

Multiple Status Codes:
- Multiple success/error schemas for different status codes
- HttpApiEndpoint.addSuccess(endpoint, { status: 201, body: Schema })

Since: effect v4 beta

name: Effect v4 Doc: HttpApi - Error Handling, Middleware, Security
episode_body:
Title: HttpApi Error Handling, Middleware, Security, and OpenAPI
Category: documentation
Source: HTTPAPI.md (Section 5)
Module Path: effect/unstable/httpapi

Description: HttpApi v4 provides structured error handling, composable middleware, security schemes, and automatic OpenAPI documentation generation.

Key Concepts:

Error Handling:
- HttpApiEndpoint.addError(endpoint, { status: 404, body: NotFoundSchema })
- Errors defined at endpoint, group, or API level
- Schema.TaggedErrorClass for typed error responses
- Predefined error types with appropriate HTTP status mapping

Middleware:
- HttpApiBuilder.middleware(handler) for API-level middleware
- Group-level and endpoint-level middleware supported
- Middleware can modify request context, add headers, handle auth
- Composable: multiple middleware layers stack

Security:
- HttpApiSecurity.apiKey for API key auth
- HttpApiSecurity.basic for Basic auth
- HttpApiSecurity.bearer for Bearer token auth
- Security applied at endpoint or group level
- Implementation via HttpApiBuilder.security handler

Services:
- Handlers can depend on Effect services
- Services provided via Layer composition
- HttpApiBuilder.group(Api, "Group", handlers => handlers.handle("endpoint", impl))
- impl can use Effect.gen with service access

OpenAPI:
- HttpApiScalar.layer(Api) adds Scalar API documentation UI
- Automatic OpenAPI 3.x specification generation from API definition
- Schema annotations (title, description, examples) map to OpenAPI metadata
- Available at /docs endpoint by default

Client Derivation:
- HttpApiClient.make(Api, { baseUrl }) derives type-safe client
- Client methods match endpoint names
- Requires FetchHttpClient.layer or custom HTTP client

Web Handler:
- HttpRouter.toWebHandler(layer) converts API to standard Request -> Response handler
- Useful for integrating with existing HTTP servers (not NodeHttpServer)

Since: effect v4 beta

name: Effect v4 Doc: ConfigProvider - Environment, JSON, DotEnv, FileSystem
episode_body:
Title: ConfigProvider: Environment Variables, JSON, DotEnv, and FileSystem Configuration
Category: documentation
Source: CONFIG.md (Section 1)
Module Path: effect/ConfigProvider

Description: The ConfigProvider module provides a unified interface for reading configuration from various sources including environment variables, JSON objects, .env files, and filesystem directories.

Key Concepts:

Core APIs:
- provider.load(["path", "segments"]) for configuration lookup
- ConfigProvider.nested("prefix")(provider) adds prefix to all paths
- ConfigProvider.mapInput(fn)(provider) transforms paths before lookup
- ConfigProvider.orElse(primary, fallback) for fallback chains
- ConfigProvider.constantCase(provider) transforms to CONSTANT_CASE

fromEnv (Environment Variables):
- ConfigProvider.fromEnv({ env: {...} }) or ConfigProvider.fromEnv() for process.env
- Mini-language: __ (double underscore) separates path segments
- Arrays: SERVERS__0=a, SERVERS__1=b (dense, canonical indices)
- Objects: DATABASE__HOST=localhost, DATABASE__PORT=5432
- Mixed: node as both leaf and container (DATABASE=default + DATABASE__HOST=localhost)
- Empty containers: __TYPE=A (empty array), __TYPE=O (empty object)
- Validation: duplicate leafs must be identical, arrays must be dense

fromDotEnv:
- ConfigProvider.fromDotEnv(content, { expandVariables: true })
- Supports comments (#), export keyword, quoted values
- Same mini-language as fromEnv

fromJson:
- ConfigProvider.fromJson({ database: { host: "localhost" } })
- Auto-converts primitives to strings

fromStringLeafJson:
- ConfigProvider.fromStringLeafJson({ database: { host: "localhost" } })
- All leaf values must already be strings

fromDir (Filesystem):
- ConfigProvider.fromDir(path) reads directory tree
- Regular files -> leaf values (content trimmed)
- Directories -> object keys
- I/O errors -> SourceError

Since: effect v4 beta

name: Effect v4 Doc: Config - Schema-Based Configuration
episode_body:
Title: Config.schema: Type-Safe Configuration with Schema Integration
Category: documentation
Source: CONFIG.md (Section 2)
Module Path: effect/Config

Description: The Config module integrates with Schema to provide type-safe, schema-validated configuration that automatically decodes string values from environment variables into typed values.

Key Concepts:

Config.schema API:
- Config.schema(schema) reads config from root
- Config.schema(schema, "path") reads from string path
- Config.schema(schema, ["a", "b"]) reads from array path
- Schema handles string-to-type decoding automatically (e.g., String -> Int, String -> URL)

Usage Pattern:
```
const config = Config.schema(Schema.Struct({
  API_KEY: Schema.String,
  PORT: Schema.Int,
  LOCALHOST: Schema.URL
}))

const program = Effect.gen(function*() {
  const c = yield* config  // typed { API_KEY: string, PORT: number, LOCALHOST: URL }
}).pipe(Effect.provide(ConfigProvider.layer(provider)))
```

Error Handling:
- SchemaError for validation failures (Formatter.makeTree().format(e.issue))
- SourceError for provider read failures

Layer Integration:
- Effect.provide(ConfigProvider.layer(provider)) provides config to program
- ConfigProvider.ConfigProvider service tag for dependency injection

Since: effect v4 beta

name: Effect v4 Doc: MCP Server - Model Context Protocol Implementation
episode_body:
Title: McpServer: MCP (Model Context Protocol) Server Implementation
Category: documentation
Source: MCP.md
Module Path: effect/unstable/ai

Description: The McpServer module implements an MCP (Model Context Protocol) server using Effect's ecosystem, enabling AI agent tool calling, resource access, and prompt management.

Key Concepts:

Imports:
- `import { McpServer, Tool, Toolkit } from "effect/unstable/ai"`
- `import { McpSchema } from "effect/unstable/ai"` for parameterized resources
- `import { NodeRuntime, NodeSink, NodeStream } from "@effect/platform-node"` for stdio transport

Tools and Toolkit:
- Tool.make("ToolName", { description, parameters: { field: Schema }, success: Schema })
- Toolkit.make(tool1, tool2, ...) groups tools
- MyToolkit.toLayer({ ToolName: (params) => Effect.succeed(result) }) provides implementations
- McpServer.toolkit(toolkit) creates server toolkit layer

Resources:
- McpServer.resource({ uri, name, description, mimeType, content: Effect }) for static resources
- McpServer.resource`file://path/${param}`({...}) for parameterized URI templates
- McpSchema.param("name", Schema) for URI template parameters
- completion field for auto-completion of parameter values
- audience: ["assistant", "user"] for access control

Prompts:
- McpServer.prompt({ name, description, parameters: { field: Schema }, completion: {}, content: (params) => Effect })
- Parameters with auto-completion support
- Content function generates prompt text from parameters

Elicitation:
- McpServer.elicit({ message, schema }) requests structured input from user
- Effect.catchTag("ElicitationDeclined", handler) for fallback on decline

Server Transport:
- McpServer.layerStdio({ name, version, stdin: NodeStream.stdin, stdout: NodeSink.stdout }) for stdio
- McpServer.layerHttp for HTTP-based transport
- Logger.layer([Logger.consolePretty({ stderr: true })]) essential for stdio (stdout is protocol)

Server Launch:
- Layer.mergeAll(resources, prompts, toolkitLayer).pipe(Layer.provide(serverLayer))
- Layer.launch(serverLayer).pipe(NodeRuntime.runMain)

Since: effect v4 beta

name: Effect v4 Doc: Optic - Functional Optics for Immutable Data
episode_body:
Title: Optic: Iso, Lens, Prism, Optional, and Traversal for Immutable Data
Category: documentation
Source: OPTIC.md
Module Path: effect/Optic

Description: The Optic module provides composable functional optics for reading and updating parts of immutable data structures safely, including Iso, Lens, Prism, Optional, and Traversal.

Key Concepts:

Optic Types:
- Iso: reversible lossless conversion between two types
- Lens: focus on a field that is always present
- Prism: focus on one case of a union
- Optional: focus that might or might not exist
- Traversal: focus on zero or more items in a collection
- All unified as Optional internally (single data type)

Core Operations:
- optic.get(data) reads focused value
- optic.replace(newValue, data) returns updated copy
- optic.modify(fn)(data) applies function to focused value
- optic.getResult(data) returns Result for debugging focus failures

Building Optics:
- Optic.id<S>() starts with identity on type S
- .key("fieldName") accesses struct field (Lens)
- .key(0) accesses tuple element
- .optionalKey("field") accesses optional field, removes key when setting undefined
- .at("key") accesses record or array index (Optional)
- .tag("VariantName") focuses union variant by _tag (Prism)
- .pick(["a", "c"]) accesses group of struct keys
- .omit(["b"]) accesses all keys except specified

Traversal:
- .forEach((item) => item.check(filter)) traverses collection with filter
- .modifyAll(fn)(data) modifies all focused items
- .notUndefined() filters out undefined values
- Traversal modeled as Optional<S, ReadonlyArray<A>>

Schema Integration:
- Schema.toIso(schema) generates Iso from schema definition
- Works with Schema.Class out of the box
- Enables optic composition on schema-typed class instances

Quick Selection Guide:
| Data shape | Use |
| Always-present field | key |
| Optional field (keep undefined) | key |
| Optional field (drop undefined) | optionalKey |
| Union case | tag |
| Record or array index | at |
| Filter and update items | forEach + check/notUndefined |

Advantages Over Immer:
- Reusable focus (define path once, reuse)
- Declarative optional handling (no manual undefined checks)
- Composition (combine small optics into larger ones)
- Type-safe union handling via tag/prism

Since: effect v4 beta
